executive_summary: This report highlights strengths in open-palm explanatory gestures and distributed room engagement. Opportunities for growth include refining gesture size, opening posture between points, and reducing extended note-reading. The feedback aims to enhance presence and audience connection through concise, actionable steps.

top_strengths.title: Open-palm explanatory delivery
top_strengths.evidence: 00:00-00:15 included open-palm explanatory gestures that supported the explanation. 00:45-01:00 included open-palm explanatory gestures that supported the explanation.
top_strengths.what_to_repeat: Keep the same open-palm gesture shape when emphasizing key ideas.

top_strengths.title: Distributed room engagement
top_strengths.evidence: 00:15-00:30 showed attention moving across more than one part of the room. 00:45-01:00 showed attention moving across more than one part of the room.
top_strengths.what_to_repeat: Reuse the left-center-right room sweep that already looks natural.

strength_inventory.title: Open-palm explanatory delivery
strength_inventory.evidence: 00:00-00:15 included open-palm explanatory gestures that supported the explanation. 00:45-01:00 included open-palm explanatory gestures that supported the explanation.
strength_inventory.what_to_repeat: Keep the same open-palm gesture shape when emphasizing key ideas.

strength_inventory.title: Distributed room engagement
strength_inventory.evidence: 00:15-00:30 showed attention moving across more than one part of the room. 00:45-01:00 showed attention moving across more than one part of the room.
strength_inventory.what_to_repeat: Reuse the left-center-right room sweep that already looks natural.

priority_actions.title: Tighten the peak size of gestures
priority_actions.why_it_matters: Large bursts of motion can distract from the teaching point when they are not tightly timed.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required. 00:15-00:30 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: Keep big gestures for true emphasis and use smaller controlled hand movements elsewhere.

priority_actions.title: Open the stance between points
priority_actions.why_it_matters: A more open posture tends to read as more confident and easier to approach.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 showed a more guarded arm-and-shoulder position between teaching points. 00:30-00:45 showed a more guarded arm-and-shoulder position between teaching points.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: Let the elbows open slightly and release any folded or guarded arm positions between points.

additional_observation_inventory.title: Reduce extended note-reading
additional_observation_inventory.evidence: 00:15-00:30 repeatedly pulled attention down to notes instead of back to the room.
additional_observation_inventory.suggested_response: Check whether note glances stay brief and return to the room within the same teaching beat.

additional_observation_inventory.title: Deliberately sweep the room
additional_observation_inventory.evidence: 00:30-00:45 stayed more fixed on one part of the room than on a left-center-right sweep.
additional_observation_inventory.suggested_response: Check whether the next explanation visibly reaches more than one side of the room.

additional_observation_inventory.title: Increase room-checking behavior
additional_observation_inventory.evidence: 00:45-01:00 showed fewer quick room checks and a less visibly alert scan.
additional_observation_inventory.suggested_response: Check whether transitions now include a visible room check before the next explanation.

low_confidence_watchlist.why_watch: The signal is visible, but it is not yet sustained enough to justify real corrective feedback.

Continue to distribute your attention across the room with natural sweeps.

evidence_moments.coaching_implication: Let the elbows open slightly and release any folded or guarded arm positions between points.
